reasoning: The CBO projects customs duties will rise to 1.3% of GDP in 2026, which translates to well over $300 billion (relative to a roughly $28 trillion U.S. economy). Actual numbers from the early months of FY2026 confirm this massive surge, with the CBO noting that February 2026 customs receipts were roughly 3.5 times higher than the previous year. Even adjusting for inflation into 2023 dollars across three years (which would require less than $180 billion nominal to clear the $160 billion real threshold), the volume of revenue already recorded and projected for FY2026 vastly exceeds $160 billion. Given that we are already halfway through FY2026 (April 2026) and collections are extraordinarily high due to Trump administration tariffs enacted in 2025, the probability of exceeding the threshold is near certain.
